channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ontario premier says he needs 'to sit down as quickly as possible' with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday that he needs "to sit down as quickly as possible" with President Trump regarding tariffs. ‘We're seeing inflation happening already, and it's unnecessary. We don't need to do this. We can have the two most prosperous countries in the world.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: A cargo ship hits a tanker and they catch fire off England, with one crew member missing
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cargo ship hit a tanker transporting jet fuel for the U.S. military off eastern England on Monday, setting both vessels ablaze and sending fuel pouring into the North Sea. All but one of the 37 crew of the two ships were brought safely ashore.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Confronting backlash against women's rights, 193 nations commit to speed action on gender equality
item.description: The U.N.’s 193 member nations have made a commitment to accelerate action on more than a dozen fronts to achieve gender equality.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mark Kelly fires back at Musk over 'traitor' accusation
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) hit back at tech billionaire Elon Musk after he called Kelly a "traitor" for visiting Ukraine and demanding the United States stand with Ukrainians in their ongoing war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Hamas offering 5-to-10-year truce with Israel: US official says
item.description: Hamas offered to hand over governance of the Gaza Strip in exchange for a five-to-10-year truce in its war with Israel, President Trump’s special envoy for hostage negotiations said on Sunday. Adam Boehler told Israel Public Broadcasting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: North Korea fires missiles as US, South Korea begin their 1st joint military exercise of Trump's 2nd term
item.description: North Korea fired several ballistic missiles into the sea after South Korea and the U.S. kicked off their first major joint military exercise of President Donald Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court-martial convenes for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A court-martial convened on Monday for Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced in federal court last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents after the most consequential national security breach in years.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Witkoff says Zelensky apologized in letter to Trump
item.description: President Trump’s special envoy Steve Witkoff said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky apologized to Trump in a letter after a explosive public argument during an Oval Office meeting. “Zelensky sent a letter to the president.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: NATO chief vows 'unwavering' support for Bosnia's integrity after Serb separatist actions
item.description: SARAJEVO, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — NATO's secretary general on Monday pledged the military alliance's “unwavering” support for Bosnia's territorial integrity after a series of Bosnian Serb separatist moves raised tensions nearly 30 years after the end of a bloody war.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio confirms ICE arrest of pro-Palestinian Columbia student protest leader
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has confirmed the arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a green card-holding Columbia University graduate and pro-Palestinian activist, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is signaling that the Trump administration has finished its six-week dismantling of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A cargo ship hits a tanker and they catch fire off England, with one crew member missing
item.description: A cargo ship carrying a toxic chemical hit a tanker carrying jet fuel for the U.S. military off England, setting both vessels on fire and sending fuel pouring into the North Sea.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syria's government signs a breakthrough deal with Kurdish-led authorities in the northeast
item.description: Syria’s interim government has reached a deal with the Kurdish-led authority that controls the country’s northeast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope follows Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as recovery continues
item.description: Pasolini delivered a meditation on “The hope of eternal life,” a theme that was chosen well before Francis was admitted to Rome's Gemelli hospital on Feb. 14 with a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Carney wins Liberal Party nomination to replace Trudeau as Canada's next PM
item.description: Former Goldman Sachs executive Mark Carney won the Liberal Party primary on Sunday and will be appointed Canada's next prime minister after the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Scotland golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: A pro-Palestinian activist group has claimed responsibility for the crimes, which included defacing a building and digging up greens.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump considering pressure on Russia and seeking deal with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said he’s “strongly considering” sanctions and tariffs on Russia in hopes of forcing a settlement to the war in Ukraine. Trump also sent a letter to Iran’s leaders seeking to negotiate a nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII bomb in Paris halts Eurostar travel to London, Brussels
item.description: Eurostar announced the cancellation of all its high-speed trains connecting Paris to London and Brussels on Friday after the discovery of a World War-II era bomb near the tracks serving Eurostar’s hub in the French capital, Gare du Nord.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump envoy says Ukrainians 'brought it on themselves' after US pauses aid and intelligence sharing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine was given “fair warning” by the White House before President Donald Trump this week ordered a pause on U.S. military assistance and intelligence sharing with Kyiv, a senior administration official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 12 killed, 30 wounded in Pakistan military base attack launched by suicide bombers
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two suicide bombings breached a wall at a military base in northwestern Pakistan before other attackers stormed the compound and were repelled in violence that killed at least 12 people and wounded 30 others, according to officials and a local hospital.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada may halt electricity exports to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Premier Doug Ford has threatened to cut off electricity exports to the US if President Trump's tariffs are enacted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Timber over Tinder: German forest has connected lovers for over a century
item.description: Known as “Bräutigamseiche” in German, the Bridegroom’s Oak has a famous knothole that's been used as a mailbox since 1892.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation
